abstract: Add a table formatted in HTML to your article.
overview:
## Overview To add an HTML table to your article, you add a component with the `role` property set to `htmltable`. In the required `html` property, add the coded HTML table consisting of your data and the HTML tags that indicate rows. Make sure that all of your HTML code is enclosed in `
` tags. To change the appearance of an HTML table, you can use any of the HTML tags Apple News Format supports; for more information, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format]. You can also apply c`omponentStyles` from Apple News Format; for more information, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TableStyle]. ### Review the HTML Table Tags Apple News Format supports these tags for HTML tables: This list describes how to use the basic HTML table tags: Consider the following example HTML code: This code results in a table with two columns and two rows â€” one row is for the heading and one is for data.
rawMarkdown:
--- source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNews/adding-an-html-table crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:58Z --- # Adding an HTML Table **Article** Add a table formatted in HTML to your article. ## Overview To add an HTML table to your article, you add a component with the `role` property set to `htmltable`. In the required `html` property, add the coded HTML table consisting of your data and the HTML tags that indicate rows. Make sure that all of your HTML code is enclosed in `
` tags. To change the appearance of an HTML table, you can use any of the HTML tags Apple News Format supports; for more information, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format]. You can also apply c`omponentStyles` from Apple News Format; for more information, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TableStyle]. ### Review the HTML Table Tags Apple News Format supports these tags for HTML tables: ```html
``` This list describes how to use the basic HTML table tags: - Begin each row of the table (including the header row) with `` and end each row with ``. - Use table heading tags (`` and ``) around each element in the table header. - Put table data tags (`` and ``) around each element in a row. - Enclose a single row or group of rows in ``, ``, or `` tags. - Style your table text using supported HTML tags. See [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format]. - Use a JSON [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentStyle] that has the `tableStyle` object defined to specify table alignment, table padding, and other, nontextual table styles. Apple News Format ignores any style-related HTML attributes, such as `cellpadding`, `cellspacing`, `width`, and `alignment`. Consider the following example HTML code: ```html
First NameLast Name
JohnAppleseed
``` This code results in a table with two columns and two rows â€” one row is for the heading and one is for data. ## Tables with HTML Data - **HTMLTable**: The component for adding tables with HTML data.

items.description: The component for adding tables with HTML data.
items.name: HTMLTable

title: Adding an HTML Table
